evals.prompt: Is StartupBase worth submitting to for SEO backlinks? How does it compare to other directories?
evals.expected_output: Explains StartupBase offers a free dofollow backlink at DR39, which is on the lower end of directory backlinks. Compares with higher-DR alternatives and recommends as part of a broader submission sweep.

assertions.name: dr_value
assertions.description: States StartupBase has a dofollow backlink around DR39 and explains this is relatively low compared to other directories

assertions.name: comparison
assertions.description: Compares with at least one higher-DR directory like Uneed, Fazier, or Product Hunt to provide context

assertions.name: cumulative_value
assertions.description: Explains the cumulative backlink value argument — that even lower-DR backlinks contribute when combined with other directory submissions

evals.prompt: How do I submit my product to StartupBase and what are the requirements?
evals.expected_output: Walks through submission process and strict requirements including tech-only, custom domain, social auth, and professional website with newsletter signup.

assertions.name: submission_steps
assertions.description: Describes creating an account via social auth (Twitter, LinkedIn, or Facebook) and submitting the product

assertions.name: tech_only
assertions.description: Mentions that only technology startups are accepted — blogs, stores, courses, and consultancies are rejected

assertions.name: review_timeline
assertions.description: Notes the free review queue takes approximately 2-3 months, with a paid option to get featured faster

evals.prompt: Where does StartupBase fit in a multi-directory launch strategy?
evals.expected_output: Places StartupBase in Phase 3 (free editorial queues) alongside other slow-review directories. Recommends submitting Day 1 as a set-and-forget play.

assertions.name: phase_placement
assertions.description: Places StartupBase in the free editorial queue phase rather than instant-listing or paid fast-track phases

assertions.name: submit_early
assertions.description: Recommends submitting early (Day 1) since the queue is long and it can run in parallel with other submissions

assertions.name: realistic_expectations
assertions.description: Sets realistic expectations about low traffic and positions the value primarily as a backlink play, not traffic acquisition
